name: MCP Email Server
what_it_does: Email MCP server enabling AI agents to send and manage emails via SMTP/IMAP — composing and sending emails, reading inbox messages, searching email content, managing folders, and integrating standard email protocols into agent-driven communication and automation workflows without provider-specific API dependencies.
use_cases: Sending automated emails and notifications from workflow automation agents
Reading and processing incoming emails from email triage agents
Searching email inbox for specific messages from information retrieval agents
Managing email folders and labels from productivity agents
Sending reports and summaries via email from reporting agents
Monitoring email for specific patterns from alerting agents
not_for: Teams needing provider-specific features (Gmail labels, Outlook calendar) — use provider MCPs
Bulk email marketing (use Mailchimp/SendGrid MCPs for mass email)
Teams preferring direct IMAP library integration over MCP layer
best_when: An agent needs generic email send/receive via SMTP/IMAP — for email automation that works across providers (Gmail, Outlook, custom SMTP) without provider-specific APIs.
avoid_when: You need Gmail-specific features, calendar integration, or are doing bulk marketing email — provider-specific MCPs or dedicated email platforms are better.

auth.notes: SMTP/IMAP credentials required: server, port, username, password (or app password). Gmail requires app-specific password if 2FA enabled. Credentials stored as environment variables.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free — uses your existing email provider; no MCP-level costs
pricing.paid_tiers: None for the MCP server; email provider charges apply
pricing.notes: MCP server is free and open source. Email sending costs depend on your SMTP provider (Gmail free, SendGrid has usage tiers, etc.).

performance.rate_limits: Depends on email provider SMTP limits (Gmail: 500/day free, etc.)

known_agent_gotchas: SMTP credentials in environment — use app-specific passwords, not main account password
Gmail and other providers may block SMTP access without enabling 'less secure apps' or app passwords
Email sending is irreversible — confirm recipients before sending from agent workflows
IMAP connection management can be finicky — connection timeouts need handling
Community MCP — basic SMTP/IMAP implementation; may lack advanced features
SMTP rate limits vary by provider — implement backoff for bulk sends
agent_readiness.security_notes: Store SMTP/IMAP credentials as environment variables — never hardcode. Use app-specific passwords, not main account passwords. TLS/STARTTLS required. Community MCP. Email content may contain sensitive information.

security_score_components.security_notes: Use app-specific passwords. TLS required. Store credentials as env vars. Community MCP. Email content may be sensitive.
